title: AR Claim
description: A claim is a verifiable structural property over a bundle. Claims are the unit of composition. Each claim has a stable identifier, declares its shape (data-driven or custom), and may declare IR mapping metadata.

id.description: Stable identifier (e.g. AR.CLAIM.VERSIONING). Identifiers are the sole normative reference for conformance.

version.description: Semantic version of this claim. Independent of the package version.

class.description: Class per SPEC: structural (derived from formal/spec), mapping (derived from AR.MAPPING.*), engineering (export bundle integrity).

supersedes.description: Claim IDs that this claim replaces.

deprecated_by.description: Claim ID that supersedes this one. Only set when status == 'deprecated' or 'superseded'.

derives_from.description: Upstream identifiers (SE.*, AR.*, JR.*, IR.*, etc.) from which this claim derives its authority.

summary.description: One-sentence description of what the claim verifies.

ir.description: Optional IR mapping. Present when the claim is about identity-regime structure. Absent claims work fine in IR-naive domains.

applies_to_families.description: IR families this claim is about.

applies_to_profiles.description: IR profile kinds this claim is about.

enforces_separation.description: IR profile pairs whose collapse this claim prevents (e.g. ['REC', 'NOR_S']).

check.description: How the claim is verified. Either declares a shape (data-driven) OR declares custom (hand-written Python).

check_bundle_field.description: Shape A: predicate over bundle-level fields. Used for conformance declarations, version declarations, manifest presence.

field.description: Dotted path into the bundle, e.g. 'versions.accountable_record'.

check_record_field.description: Shape B: predicate over records of declared types. Used for required-field, forbidden-field, and category-separation checks.

applies_to_record_types.description: Record types this claim applies to. If empty, applies to all records.

check_cross_reference.description: Shape C: cross-record reference resolution. Used for source traceability and ref-resolvability checks.

required.description: If false, missing reference is acceptable; only unresolvable references fail.

check_trait_conformance.description: Trait conformance check. Verifies that record types declared to conform to a trait actually satisfy its requirements. Composition, not inheritance: partial conformance is meaningful.

trait_id.description: Trait identifier (e.g. AR.TRAIT.NOR_S) whose conformance is checked.

check_custom.description: Escape hatch: claim is implemented as a Python class subclassing CustomClaim. Used when the property cannot be reduced to a declarative shape.

implementation.description: Dotted import path to a class subclassing accountable_record.claims.custom.CustomClaim.
